name: Stir Fried Caribou with Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 8 oz Caribou, deer or beef steak
1/2 medium Onion, sliced into crescent fans
recipeInstructions:
To make sauce: Soak the beans in water overnight. Rinch and drain them. Combine the black beans, stock, ginger, garlic, chile pepper, and vinegar in a saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, until beans are tender (about 1 hour). Pour into a blender or food processor, add salt, and blend until smooth. Return to the saucepan and keep warm over low heat.
To make stir-fry: Trim all fat from meat and cut against the grain into quarter-inch strips. Heat oil in a nonstick skillet or wok over high heat. Add meat and stir fry for a few seconds. Add ginger, garlic, celery, mushrooms, and onion; stir fry until vegetables are tender-crisp. Add sprouts and pour 1 cup of sauce over ingredients. Cover and cook for another 30-40 seconds. Remove from heat. Serve with steamed rice and pass the remaining sauce at the table.
